content:
Route a user message by its data sensitivity. Work through these checks IN ORDER and stop at the first match (highest sensitivity wins):

1. Any secret, credential, or regulated identifier (API key, password, private key, token, SSN, credit-card/bank number)? -> "restricted"
2. Any personal or customer data / PII (a name with contact info, email/postal address, health data, performance review, customer record)? -> "confidential"
3. Any non-public organizational context that isn't secret (internal docs, roadmaps, team plans, policies)? -> "internal"
4. Otherwise — general questions, public facts, ordinary code, no sensitive data -> "public"

Classify by the DATA actually present, not by whether the request seems harmless. Respond with ONLY: {"sensitivity": "public" | "internal" | "confidential" | "restricted"}
